nodes.name: companies-spec.md
nodes.summary: Formal specification for the agent company package format, covering directory layout, frontmatter fields, and required sections for COMPANY.md, AGENTS.md, TEAM.md, PROJECT.md, and TASK.md files.

nodes.name: example-company.md
nodes.summary: Worked example of a complete agent company package showing directory structure and content for COMPANY.md, multiple AGENTS.md files (CEO, CTO, engineer roles), TEAM.md, PROJECT.md, TASK.md, and a skill with an external reference.

nodes.name: from-repo-guide.md
nodes.summary: Guide for generating an agent company package from an existing code repository, covering analysis steps, handling pre-existing skills and agent configurations, and common repo pattern templates for CLI, library, full-app, and skills-collection projects.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Agent skill definition for the Company Creator, which interviews users and generates a fully structured Paperclip agent company package either from scratch or from an existing repository, with detailed steps for gathering context, generating files, and handling .paperclip.yaml adapter rules.
nodes.languageNotes: References companion reference files companies-spec.md, example-company.md, and from-repo-guide.md as input to the generation process.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive developer guide for creating a new Paperclip agent adapter, covering architecture overview, shared type interfaces from @paperclipai/adapter-utils, step-by-step module creation (server, UI, CLI), registration checklists, session management, security considerations, and a minimal adapter checklist.
nodes.languageNotes: Covers the three-layer adapter structure (server/execute.ts, ui/parse-stdout.ts, cli/format-event.ts) and the registration pattern across server, UI, and CLI registries.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step agent skill for handling security vulnerability disclosures confidentially, including fetching advisory details, creating a temporary private fork, developing the fix, coordinating with reporters, requesting a CVE, and publishing the fix and advisory simultaneously.

nodes.name: audit-checklist.md
nodes.summary: Structured audit checklist used by the doc-maintenance agent to verify that README.md, doc/SPEC.md, and doc/PRODUCT.md remain accurate and complete after code changes.

nodes.name: section-map.md
nodes.summary: Mapping document that records which sections of documentation correspond to which parts of the codebase, used by the doc-maintenance agent to target precise edits.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Agent skill for keeping project documentation synchronized with code changes, with a seven-step workflow covering change detection, classification, summarization, per-document auditing, branch creation with minimal edits, PR opening, and cursor update.

nodes.name: html-report-starter.html
nodes.summary: Starter HTML template for the PR Report skill providing a pre-styled, self-contained single-page report layout with dark/light theme support, typography, color tokens, and placeholder sections for agents to fill in PR analysis content.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-contained HTML with embedded CSS and minimal JavaScript; designed to be populated by agents generating PR analysis reports.

nodes.name: style-guide.md
nodes.summary: Design style guide for PR report artifacts, specifying visual direction, typography scale, layout conventions, color palette, useful UI elements, motion guidance, and content presentation rules that agents must follow when generating HTML reports.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Agent skill for performing deep pull request reviews and producing a styled HTML artifact, with an eight-step workflow covering target framing, system mental-model building, maintainer-perspective review, objection classification, external comparison, actionable recommendations, artifact construction, and pre-handoff verification.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Agent skill for monitoring and fixing GitHub Actions CI checks on a pull request in a loop, tracking head SHAs, waiting for checks to run, investigating and fixing failures, pushing fixes, and escalating unresolvable blockers.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Agent skill for generating structured release changelogs using a calver versioning model, with steps for idempotency checking, stable range determination, raw input gathering, breaking-change detection, user-facing categorization with contributor attribution, and final file writing.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: End-to-end release coordination skill for Paperclip, guiding an agent through candidate selection, changelog drafting, SHA verification, canary validation and smoke testing, stable publication to npm, Docker, and GitHub Releases, plus failure handling and output summary.

nodes.name: component-index.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive index of all Paperclip UI components covering shadcn/ui primitives, custom components (StatusBadge, EntityRow, MetricCard, etc.), layout components, dialog and form components, agent configuration components, and utility hooks with usage examples for each.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Paperclip frontend design guide for Claude Code, specifying design principles, tech stack, design tokens (colors, radius, shadows), typography scale, status and priority systems, component hierarchy rules, composition patterns, interactive patterns, layout system, and the /design-guide showcase page conventions.

nodes.name: company-creator
nodes.summary: Symlink or stub entry pointing to the company-creator skill for use by the Claude Code harness.

nodes.name: paperclip
nodes.summary: Symlink or stub entry pointing to the Paperclip skill for use by the Claude Code harness.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build context exclusion list preventing node_modules, local environment files, and development artifacts from being copied into the Docker image.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Example environment variable template documenting required runtime configuration keys: DATABASE_URL, PORT, SERVE_UI, and BETTER_AUTH_SECRET.

nodes.name: CODEOWNERS
nodes.summary: GitHub CODEOWNERS file assigning code review ownership responsibilities to specific teams or individuals for different parts of the repository.

nodes.name: PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md
nodes.summary: GitHub pull request template enforcing a structured format covering thinking path, what changed, verification steps, risk assessment, model used, and a contributor checklist.

nodes.name: docker.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds a multi-platform Docker image (linux/amd64 and linux/arm64) and pushes it to GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io) on every push to master or version tag.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses docker/build-push-action with GHA layer caching and automatic semver/SHA tagging via docker/metadata-action.

nodes.name: e2e.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow for manually-triggered end-to-end tests using Playwright and Chromium, with an option to skip LLM-dependent assertions and automatic artifact upload of test results.

nodes.name: pr.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs on pull requests to master, enforcing lockfile hygiene, validating Dockerfile deps stage and release package manifests, running tests and type checks across the monorepo, and building the Docker image.

nodes.name: refresh-lockfile.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that automatically refreshes the pnpm lockfile on pushes to master and opens a pull request with the updated lockfile, ensuring the CI-owned lockfile stays current.

nodes.name: release-smoke.yml
nodes.summary: Reusable and manually-triggered GitHub Actions smoke test workflow that installs a published Paperclip dist-tag (canary or latest) inside a Docker container and validates the running server, uploading diagnostics as an artifact.

nodes.name: release.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions release workflow that publishes canary packages on every push to master and supports manual stable releases with date-based versioning, including Docker image publishing and downstream smoke test triggering.

nodes.name: .mailmap
nodes.summary: Git mailmap file that normalizes contributor names and email addresses for consistent attribution in git log output.

nodes.name: .npmrc
nodes.summary: npm/pnpm configuration file that sets registry or workspace preferences for the monorepo package publishing workflow.

nodes.name: adapter-plugin.md
nodes.summary: Developer guide explaining how to build custom adapter plugins for Paperclip, covering the adapter interface contract, plugin registration, and integration patterns for connecting external AI agent runtimes.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Automated changelog for the paperclipai CLI package tracking patch and minor releases from v0.2.1 through v0.3.1 with detailed per-version change descriptions.

nodes.name: esbuild.config.mjs
nodes.summary: esbuild configuration for bundling the paperclipai CLI into a single ESM file, inlining all internal @paperclipai workspace packages while keeping external npm dependencies as runtime requirements.
nodes.languageNotes: Dynamically reads each workspace package.json to build the external dependency list, distinguishing between bundled workspace packages and runtime-resolved ones like @paperclipai/server.

nodes.name: agent-jwt-env.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying that agent JWT environment variable injection works correctly when the CLI reads configuration, ensuring auth tokens are properly propagated to agent processes.

nodes.name: allowed-hostname.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests that validate the allowed-hostname allowlist feature in CLI configuration, verifying that hostname validation is correctly enforced from config file settings.

nodes.name: auth-command-registration.test.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal test that verifies the auth command is correctly registered in the CLI command tree and appears in the help output.

nodes.name: board-auth.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the board authentication flow in the CLI, verifying that auth credentials are read from and written to the correct file paths.

nodes.name: common.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for shared CLI utility functions covering common helpers used across multiple commands, using temp directories to isolate file system side effects.

nodes.name: company-delete.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the company delete CLI command, verifying correct deletion behavior and error handling against a mock company fixture.

nodes.name: company-import-export-e2e.test.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive end-to-end tests for the company import and export CLI commands, spinning up a real Paperclip server against a test PostgreSQL database and exercising the full roundtrip of importing and exporting company data.
nodes.languageNotes: Spawns child processes for both the server and CLI, dynamically finds available ports, and polls for server readiness — a heavyweight integration harness that tests real CLI/server interaction.

nodes.name: getAvailablePort
nodes.summary: Finds a free TCP port by creating a temporary server, binding to port 0, and resolving with the OS-assigned port number before closing.

nodes.name: writeTestConfig
nodes.summary: Generates a complete Paperclip server configuration JSON file at a temp path, wiring together port, database connection string, and path overrides needed for isolated e2e test runs.

nodes.name: createServerEnv
nodes.summary: Builds the process environment object for spawning the Paperclip server in tests, stripping conflicting env vars and injecting the config path, port, and database connection string.

nodes.name: runCliJson
nodes.summary: Executes the CLI binary as a child process with given args and returns parsed JSON from stdout, handling the JSON output boundary within mixed stdout streams.

nodes.name: waitForServer
nodes.summary: Polls the server health endpoint repeatedly until it responds successfully or a timeout expires, providing test readiness synchronization for the spawned server process.

nodes.name: company-import-url.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for importing a company from a remote URL via the CLI, verifying that URL-based import correctly fetches and processes the remote company definition.

nodes.name: company-import-zip.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the zip-based company import CLI command, verifying that local zip archives are correctly unpacked and their contents loaded as company definitions.

nodes.name: company.test.ts
nodes.summary: Extensive unit and integration tests for the company CLI commands, covering listing, creating, updating, and inspecting companies with a wide range of scenarios and edge cases.

nodes.name: context.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for CLI context management, verifying that context configuration is correctly read from and written to isolated temp directories.

nodes.name: data-dir.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the CLI data directory resolution logic, ensuring correct paths are computed across different operating systems and configuration states.

nodes.name: doctor.test.ts
nodes.summary: Tests for the CLI doctor command, which validates the health of a Paperclip configuration and reports issues with missing or misconfigured fields.

nodes.name: createTempConfig
nodes.summary: Builds a complete temporary Paperclip configuration directory tree with all required config files, used as the baseline fixture for doctor command test cases.

nodes.name: env-lab.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite for CLI environment lab functionality, verifying environment variable parsing and isolation behavior.

nodes.name: feedback.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite for the CLI feedback command, using a makeTrace helper to construct trace fixtures that simulate agent execution traces sent as feedback.

nodes.name: makeTrace
nodes.summary: Factory function that constructs a complete agent trace fixture with sensible defaults, accepting overrides for test parameterization.

nodes.name: embedded-postgres.ts
nodes.summary: Re-export barrel for embedded PostgreSQL test support utilities, providing helper functions and types used by integration tests that require a real database.

nodes.name: zip.ts
nodes.summary: Test helper that implements a minimal ZIP archive builder in pure TypeScript, used to create plugin zip fixtures for CLI tests without external dependencies.

nodes.name: createStoredZipArchive
nodes.summary: Builds a stored (uncompressed) ZIP archive from a map of filename-to-content entries, producing a valid binary buffer conforming to the ZIP specification.

nodes.name: home-paths.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite validating home directory path resolution utilities used by the CLI to locate configuration, data, and runtime files.

nodes.name: http.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite for the HTTP CLI adapter, verifying event formatting and output behavior when the CLI communicates with the Paperclip server over HTTP.

nodes.name: plugin-init.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite for the CLI plugin initialization command, verifying scaffold generation into temporary directories with cleanup between tests.

nodes.name: routines.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for the CLI routines command, using a writeTestConfig helper to set up complete config fixtures before verifying routine scheduling and execution.

nodes.name: writeTestConfig
nodes.summary: Writes a complete Paperclip config JSON fixture to disk for routines integration tests, wiring up agents, services, and database connection strings.

nodes.name: secrets.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite for the CLI secrets management commands, using factory helpers to construct agent, secret, and config fixtures with configurable secrets providers.

nodes.name: configWithSecretsProvider
nodes.summary: Factory function that builds a full Paperclip config fixture pre-wired with a specific secrets provider, used to test secrets resolution across different provider backends.

nodes.name: telemetry.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite for CLI telemetry opt-in/opt-out behavior, using a makeConfigPath helper to create config fixtures with telemetry enabled or disabled.

nodes.name: makeConfigPath
nodes.summary: Creates a temporary Paperclip config directory on disk with telemetry enabled or disabled, returning the config path for use in telemetry tests.

nodes.name: worktree-merge-history.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite for worktree merge history logic, providing factory helpers for issues, comments, document revisions, attachments, projects, and project workspaces to build complex merge scenario fixtures.

nodes.name: format-event.ts
nodes.summary: Formats raw HTTP stdout events from the Paperclip server for CLI display, trimming whitespace before printing to console.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Entry point for the HTTP CLI adapter module, exporting the httpCLIAdapter implementation used when the CLI communicates with the server over HTTP.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export for the CLI adapters module, exposing getCLIAdapter and the CLIAdapterModule type to callers that need to select an adapter at runtime.

nodes.name: format-event.ts
nodes.summary: Formats raw process stdout events from agent subprocesses for CLI display, trimming whitespace before printing to console.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Entry point for the process CLI adapter module, exporting the processCLIAdapter implementation used when the CLI manages agents as local subprocesses.

nodes.name: agent-jwt-secret-check.ts
nodes.summary: Pre-flight check that validates and ensures the agent JWT secret is present — reading from env vars or an env file, then generating a new secret if absent.

nodes.name: agentJwtSecretCheck
nodes.summary: Runs the agent JWT secret pre-flight check: reads from env or env file, reports status, and auto-generates a secret when none exists.

nodes.name: config-check.ts
nodes.summary: Pre-flight check that verifies the Paperclip configuration file exists and is valid, reporting errors and the resolved config path to the user.

nodes.name: configCheck
nodes.summary: Resolves and validates the CLI config file, returning a structured check result with pass/fail status and descriptive error messages.

nodes.name: database-check.ts
nodes.summary: Pre-flight check that verifies database connectivity and ensures required runtime directories exist, dynamically importing the Drizzle DB module to test the connection.

nodes.name: databaseCheck
nodes.summary: Runs the database pre-flight check: dynamically imports the DB driver, executes a test query, resolves runtime paths, and creates missing directories.

nodes.name: deployment-auth-check.ts
nodes.summary: Pre-flight check that verifies authentication secrets required for public deployment — validating BETTER_AUTH_SECRET and PAPERCLIP_AGENT_JWT_SECRET env vars based on the inferred bind mode.

nodes.name: deploymentAuthCheck
nodes.summary: Checks that auth-related env vars are set when deploying publicly, returning detailed check results per secret with appropriate warnings for remote bind mode.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export for all CLI pre-flight check functions, consolidating agentJwtSecretCheck, configCheck, databaseCheck, deploymentAuthCheck, llmCheck, logCheck, portCheck, secretsCheck, and storageCheck into a single import surface.

nodes.name: llm-check.ts
nodes.summary: Validates LLM provider connectivity and configuration during CLI startup checks, making test requests to configured LLM endpoints to confirm they are reachable and responding.

nodes.name: llmCheck
nodes.summary: Exported check function that validates LLM provider API connectivity by performing test HTTP requests to the configured endpoint.

nodes.name: log-check.ts
nodes.summary: Pre-flight check that verifies the configured log directory exists (creating it if needed) and is writable before the server starts.

nodes.name: logCheck
nodes.summary: Verifies the log directory is accessible, creating it if absent, and reports errors if write permission is denied.

nodes.name: path-resolver.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal re-export shim that exposes the resolveRuntimeLikePath utility for use across the checks module.

nodes.name: port-check.ts
nodes.summary: Pre-flight check that verifies the configured server port is available before startup, preventing bind errors at runtime.

nodes.name: portCheck
nodes.summary: Checks whether the port specified in the server config is available and reports a clear error if it is already in use.

nodes.name: secrets-check.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive pre-flight check that validates master encryption key configuration — supporting env-var, key-file, and AWS Secrets Manager sources — and auto-generates a local key file when none is configured.

nodes.name: secretsCheck
nodes.summary: Main exported check that resolves the master encryption key from multiple sources, auto-generates it if absent, and enforces strict-mode file permission requirements.

nodes.name: awsSecretsManagerCheck
nodes.summary: Validates that all required AWS Secrets Manager configuration environment variables are present and that at least one AWS credential source is detected.

nodes.name: storage-check.ts
nodes.summary: Pre-flight check that validates the configured storage backend — verifying local directory existence and write access for local storage, or S3 bucket and region configuration for S3 storage.

nodes.name: storageCheck
nodes.summary: Validates storage configuration by checking directory write access for local mode or required bucket/region fields for S3 mode.

nodes.name: board-auth.ts
nodes.summary: Manages persistent CLI authentication credentials for board connections — handles reading/writing a local auth store, OAuth-style device login flow with browser URL opening and token polling, and credential revocation.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a polling-based device auth flow similar to OAuth device code grant, storing credentials keyed by normalized API base URL.

nodes.name: loginBoardCli
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full interactive login flow: requests a login URL from the server, opens it in the browser, polls for token completion, and persists the resulting credential.

nodes.name: readBoardAuthStore
nodes.summary: Reads and normalizes the local JSON auth store file, returning a default empty store if the file does not exist.

nodes.name: setStoredBoardCredential
nodes.summary: Persists a board credential (token, user info, company) into the local auth store keyed by the normalized API base URL.

nodes.name: command-label.ts
nodes.summary: Tiny utility that builds a human-readable label from the current process argv, used to identify which CLI command initiated a login session.

nodes.name: context.ts
nodes.summary: Manages the CLI client context file which stores named connection profiles (API base URL, company ID, API key env var); provides read, write, upsert, and profile-switching operations.

nodes.name: readContext
nodes.summary: Reads and normalizes the client context file from disk, returning a default context when the file is absent.

nodes.name: upsertProfile
nodes.summary: Creates or updates a named connection profile within the context file, merging the provided patch fields into the existing profile data.

nodes.name: normalizeContext
nodes.summary: Normalizes raw parsed context JSON into a well-typed ClientContext object, sanitizing profile entries and current profile pointer.

nodes.name: http.ts
nodes.summary: Core HTTP client module providing the PaperclipApiClient class and typed error classes used by all CLI commands to communicate with the Paperclip server API.
nodes.languageNotes: PaperclipApiClient implements automatic auth recovery via a recoverAuth callback that retries the request after re-authenticating.

nodes.name: PaperclipApiClient
nodes.summary: HTTP client wrapping fetch with typed get/post/patch/delete methods, automatic JSON serialization, bearer-token auth, run-ID headers, and recoverable auth retry logic.

nodes.name: ApiRequestError
nodes.summary: Typed error for non-2xx HTTP responses, carrying status code, structured details array, and raw response body.

nodes.name: ApiConnectionError
nodes.summary: Typed error for network-level connection failures, enriched with URL, HTTP method, and formatted cause message.

nodes.name: allowed-hostname.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the CLI command that adds a new allowed hostname to the server configuration, normalizing and deduplicating the hostname list before persisting.

nodes.name: addAllowedHostname
nodes.summary: Reads the current server config, normalizes and deduplicates the allowed hostnames list, appends the new host, and writes back the updated config.

nodes.name: auth-bootstrap-ceo.ts
nodes.summary: CLI command that bootstraps an initial CEO invite token directly in the database, enabling first-time admin onboarding for a fresh Paperclip instance.

nodes.name: bootstrapCeoInvite
nodes.summary: Creates a bootstrap CEO invite token by connecting directly to the database, revoking any existing active bootstrap invites, inserting a new time-limited invite, and printing the sign-up URL.

nodes.name: activity.ts
nodes.summary: Registers the `activity` subcommand group for the CLI client, currently providing a `list` command to query and display company activity log entries with optional filters.

nodes.name: registerActivityCommands
nodes.summary: Attaches the activity command group and its list subcommand to the commander program, wiring options and API calls.

nodes.name: agent.ts
nodes.summary: Registers all `agent` subcommands for the CLI client, including installing Claude/Codex skills and managing agent lifecycle operations against the Paperclip API.

nodes.name: registerAgentCommands
nodes.summary: Attaches the full agent command group (list, get, pause, resume, run, skills install) to the commander program with all options and API wiring.

nodes.name: installSkillsForTarget
nodes.summary: Installs Claude or Codex skill files into the target directory, creating .claude/skills or .codex/skills subdirectories as appropriate.

nodes.name: approval.ts
nodes.summary: Registers the `approval` subcommand group for the CLI client, providing commands to list pending approvals and submit approval or rejection responses.

nodes.name: registerApprovalCommands
nodes.summary: Attaches approval list and respond subcommands to the commander program, wiring interactive prompts and API calls for human-in-the-loop approval workflows.

nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: Registers the `auth` subcommand group for the CLI client, handling login and logout flows that persist board credentials to the local auth store.

nodes.name: common.ts
nodes.summary: Shared utilities for all CLI client commands — resolves connection context and API credentials, adds common options to commands, formats output, and handles errors consistently.

nodes.name: resolveCommandContext
nodes.summary: Resolves the active connection profile and constructs a PaperclipApiClient for a command, supporting API key env vars, board auth token recovery, and config-file API base inference.

nodes.name: printOutput
nodes.summary: Formats and prints API response data to stdout as either pretty-printed JSON or a tabular/inline record view based on output format options.

nodes.name: handleCommandError
nodes.summary: Central error handler for CLI commands that formats ApiRequestError and ApiConnectionError messages and exits with a non-zero code.

nodes.name: company.ts
nodes.summary: Largest CLI command module implementing all company management subcommands — import/export, creation, deletion, agent attachment, and GitHub source integration — with rich interactive selection prompts and progress rendering.

nodes.name: registerCompanyCommands
nodes.summary: Registers all company subcommands (list, get, create, import, export, delete, agent, context) with the commander program, wiring options, prompts, and API calls.

nodes.name: renderCompanyImportPreview
nodes.summary: Renders a rich terminal preview of the company import plan — listing agents, projects, context files, and inline secrets with action chips and preview examples.

nodes.name: promptForImportSelection
nodes.summary: Interactive multi-select prompt that lets users choose which agents, projects, and files to include in a company import operation.

nodes.name: normalizeGithubImportSource
nodes.summary: Normalizes various GitHub import source formats (shorthand org/repo, full URLs, branch specs) into a canonical import URL.

nodes.name: resolveCompanyForDeletion
nodes.summary: Resolves the target company for deletion by ID or interactive selection, fetching all companies when no ID is supplied and prompting the user to choose.

nodes.name: context.ts
nodes.summary: Registers the `context` subcommand group for managing named CLI connection profiles — enabling profile listing, switching, and creation via interactive prompts.

nodes.name: dashboard.ts
nodes.summary: Registers the `dashboard` command that opens the Paperclip web dashboard URL for the current connection profile in the system browser.

nodes.name: feedback.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive feedback trace management module implementing commands to list, export, and summarize LLM feedback traces, including ZIP archive creation and bundle download with inline CRC32/ZIP implementation.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains a hand-rolled CRC32 and stored ZIP archive writer to avoid binary dependencies for the export bundle feature.

nodes.name: registerFeedbackCommands
nodes.summary: Registers all feedback subcommands (list, export, show, vote) to the commander program with filtering options and output format selection.

nodes.name: writeFeedbackExportBundle
nodes.summary: Fetches, decompresses, and writes a feedback trace export bundle to a local directory, extracting individual JSON trace files with progress reporting.

nodes.name: createStoredZipArchive
nodes.summary: Creates a stored (uncompressed) ZIP archive from an array of named file entries using a hand-rolled ZIP format implementation.

nodes.name: issue.ts
nodes.summary: Registers the `issue` subcommand group providing CRUD operations for Paperclip issues — list, get, create, update, and delete — with filtering, sorting, and CSV field parsing support.

nodes.name: registerIssueCommands
nodes.summary: Attaches all issue CRUD subcommands to the commander program with extensive options for filtering, sorting, pagination, and field selection.

nodes.name: plugin.ts
nodes.summary: Registers the `plugin` subcommand group for managing Paperclip plugins — install, update, list, uninstall, and init scaffolding — with support for local path and npm package sources.

nodes.name: registerPluginCommands
nodes.summary: Attaches all plugin management subcommands (list, install, update, uninstall, init) with options for local paths, registry sources, and interactive prompts.

nodes.name: buildPluginInstallRequest
nodes.summary: Constructs the API request payload for a plugin installation, resolving local path or package sources and validating the input.

nodes.name: secrets.ts
nodes.summary: Registers the `secrets` subcommand group for managing agent secret declarations — list, get, set, delete, migrate inline env vars — with provider health checking and sensitive-value redaction.

nodes.name: registerSecretCommands
nodes.summary: Attaches all secrets subcommands to the commander program, including list, get, set, delete, health, and migrate-inline-env with appropriate options.

nodes.name: migrateInlineEnv
nodes.summary: Migrates inline environment variables from agent configs to the secrets store, with interactive confirmation and dry-run support.

nodes.name: collectInlineSecretMigrationCandidates
nodes.summary: Scans agent configurations for sensitive inline environment variable values that are candidates for migration to the secrets store.

nodes.name: zip.ts
nodes.summary: Provides ZIP archive reading utilities used by the client to decompress and extract portable export bundles downloaded from the server.

nodes.name: readZipArchive
nodes.summary: Parses a ZIP archive buffer by reading the central directory and inflating individual file entries into named byte arrays.

nodes.name: configure.ts
nodes.summary: Interactive CLI command for configuring a Paperclip instance, walking the user through database, LLM, logging, server, storage, and secrets settings via a @clack/prompts wizard.

nodes.name: defaultConfig
nodes.summary: Builds the default configuration object by resolving instance ID, data directories, and default storage/secrets settings.

nodes.name: configure
nodes.summary: Exported command handler that drives the interactive configuration wizard, merging user inputs with defaults and persisting the resulting config file.

nodes.name: db-backup.ts
nodes.summary: CLI command for running PostgreSQL database backups, resolving the connection string and backup directory from config, then delegating to the runDatabaseBackup utility.

nodes.name: dbBackupCommand
nodes.summary: Exported command handler that orchestrates a database backup: resolves config, displays parameters, shows a spinner, runs the backup, and reports the outcome.

nodes.name: doctor.ts
nodes.summary: CLI diagnostic command that runs a suite of health checks (config, auth, secrets, storage, database, LLM, ports) and optionally auto-repairs failed checks.

nodes.name: doctor
nodes.summary: Exported command handler that sequentially runs all system checks, prints per-check results, and concludes with a summary of pass/warn/fail counts.

nodes.name: maybeRepair
nodes.summary: Prompts the user to confirm auto-repair for a failed check, executes the repair function if confirmed, and reports success or failure.

nodes.name: runRepairableCheck
nodes.summary: Runs a health check, prints the result, and conditionally triggers the repair flow and re-check cycle.

nodes.name: env-lab.ts
nodes.summary: CLI subcommands for managing the SSH env-lab fixture (isolated SSH test environment), providing up/down/status/doctor operations registered under the env-lab command group.

nodes.name: registerEnvLabCommands
nodes.summary: Registers all env-lab subcommands (up, status, down, doctor) on the Commander program instance.

nodes.name: collectEnvLabDoctorStatus
nodes.summary: Gathers SSH env-lab fixture health status by checking support availability and fixture state in parallel.

nodes.name: env.ts
nodes.summary: CLI command that collects and displays all environment variables needed for a Paperclip deployment, categorized by source (config file, defaults, computed values) with status indicators.

nodes.name: envCommand
nodes.summary: Exported command handler that reads the config, collects deployment environment rows, and renders them grouped by missing/optional/defined status.

nodes.name: collectDeploymentEnvRows
nodes.summary: Comprehensive function that maps every Paperclip config section to its corresponding environment variable entries, resolving defaults, statuses, and shell-quoting values.

nodes.name: heartbeat-run.ts

nodes.summary: Comprehensive migration creating nine plugin system tables: `plugins`, `plugin_config`, `plugin_state`, `plugin_entities`, `plugin_jobs`, `plugin_job_runs`, `plugin_webhook_deliveries`, `plugin_company_settings`, and `plugin_logs` — the full persistence layer for the extensibility framework.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes a full rollback comment block at the top, making this migration self-documented and reversible.

nodes.summary: Stores JSON configuration blob per plugin with error tracking.

nodes.name: plugin_state
nodes.summary: Key-value state store for plugins scoped by scope kind, scope ID, and namespace, allowing plugins to persist arbitrary runtime state.

nodes.name: plugin_company_settings
nodes.summary: Per-company plugin settings with enable flag, JSON config, and error tracking for multi-tenant plugin configuration.

nodes.summary: Records budget threshold breach incidents with observed vs. limit amounts, approval linkage, and resolution tracking.

nodes.name: companies (pause_reason, paused_at)
nodes.summary: Adds pause_reason and paused_at columns to the companies table, enabling company-level pause state tracking with reason and timestamp.

nodes.name: budget_incidents (policy_window_threshold_idx)
nodes.summary: Recreates the budget_incidents_policy_window_threshold_idx as a unique partial index on policy_id, window_start, and threshold_type for non-dismissed incidents.

nodes.name: execution_workspaces
nodes.summary: Introduces the execution_workspaces table that tracks AI agent workspaces for code execution, including mode, strategy, repository, branch, lifecycle timestamps, and cleanup state.

nodes.name: issue_work_products
nodes.summary: Introduces the issue_work_products table linking issues to external work outputs (pull requests, deployments) produced by agent execution workspaces, with health and review state tracking.

nodes.name: instance_settings
nodes.summary: Creates the instance_settings singleton table for storing global Paperclip instance configuration, including an experimental features JSONB column.

nodes.name: workspace_operations
nodes.summary: Creates the workspace_operations table logging individual shell commands executed within agent workspaces, capturing command phase, status, exit code, logs, and timing.

nodes.name: heartbeat_runs (process columns)
nodes.summary: Adds process_pid, process_started_at, retry_of_run_id, and process_loss_retry_count columns to heartbeat_runs, enabling process tracking and self-referential retry chains.

nodes.name: routines
nodes.summary: Creates the core routines table for recurring AI agent tasks, defining ownership, assignment, concurrency and catch-up policies, and audit timestamps.

nodes.name: routine_triggers
nodes.summary: Creates the routine_triggers table storing cron schedules, webhook signing keys, and execution state for each trigger that fires a routine.

nodes.name: routine_runs
nodes.summary: Creates the routine_runs table recording each individual execution run of a routine, including trigger source, idempotency key, coalescing, and failure details.

nodes.name: issues (open_routine_execution_uq, routine_triggers_public_id_uq)
nodes.summary: Adds a partial unique index on issues to prevent duplicate open routine-execution issues, and a unique index on routine_triggers.public_id for webhook deduplication.

nodes.name: instance_settings (general column)
nodes.summary: Adds a general JSONB column to instance_settings for storing unstructured instance-level configuration data.

nodes.name: company_skills
nodes.summary: Creates the company_skills table enabling companies to register custom skills (agent capabilities) with source locators, trust levels, and compatibility metadata.

nodes.name: issues (open_routine_execution_uq rebuild)
nodes.summary: Drops and recreates the issues_open_routine_execution_uq partial unique index with an additional condition requiring execution_run_id to be non-null.

nodes.name: board_api_keys
nodes.summary: Creates the board_api_keys table for storing hashed user API keys with expiry and revocation support.

nodes.name: cli_auth_challenges
nodes.summary: Creates the cli_auth_challenges table implementing the CLI authentication flow, capturing approval, cancellation, and expiry state for in-flight auth requests.

nodes.name: issue_inbox_archives
nodes.summary: Creates the issue_inbox_archives table tracking which users have archived specific issues from their inbox view.

nodes.name: document_revisions (title, format columns)
nodes.summary: Adds title and format columns to document_revisions and backfills them from the parent documents table, migrating per-document metadata to per-revision metadata.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an UPDATE...FROM join to backfill historical rows during migration, a common safe migration pattern.

nodes.name: feedback_votes
nodes.summary: Creates feedback_votes for capturing user votes (thumbs up/down) on agent outputs, including consent and redaction tracking for data sharing with Paperclip Labs.

nodes.name: feedback_exports
nodes.summary: Creates feedback_exports tracking the export pipeline for feedback votes shared with Paperclip Labs, including payload digest, versioning, and retry tracking.

nodes.name: routines (variables column)
nodes.summary: Adds a variables JSONB array column to routines, enabling parameterized routine definitions.

nodes.name: issue_relations
nodes.summary: Creates the issue_relations table for typed relationships between issues (e.g., blocks, duplicates), with a unique constraint preventing duplicate edges.

nodes.name: projects (env column)
nodes.summary: Adds an env JSONB column to projects for storing project-level environment variable configurations.

nodes.name: issues / issue_comments (full-text search indexes)
nodes.summary: Enables the pg_trgm extension and creates GIN trigram indexes on issues title, description, identifier, and issue_comments body for full-text search support.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pg_trgm GIN indexes for efficient ILIKE/similarity search without full-text search dictionaries.

nodes.name: issue_execution_decisions
nodes.summary: Creates the issue_execution_decisions table recording human or agent approval/rejection decisions at each stage of an issue's execution pipeline.

nodes.name: inbox_dismissals
nodes.summary: Creates the inbox_dismissals table tracking which inbox notification items a user has dismissed, keyed by a string item_key.

nodes.name: routines (nullable project_id, assignee_agent_id)
nodes.summary: Relaxes the routines table by making project_id and assignee_agent_id nullable, supporting draft routines not yet assigned to a project or agent.

nodes.name: heartbeat_runs (process_group_id column)
nodes.summary: Adds a process_group_id integer column to heartbeat_runs for grouping runs by OS process group for lifecycle management.

nodes.name: company_user_sidebar_preferences
nodes.summary: Creates tables for persisting per-user sidebar preferences including project ordering within a company and company ordering globally.

nodes.name: join_requests (deduplication indexes)
nodes.summary: Deduplicates existing join_requests rows and adds unique partial indexes to prevent duplicate pending join requests per user or email within a company.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a CTE with row_number() to identify and delete duplicate rows before creating the unique constraint, a safe deduplication migration pattern.

nodes.name: 0058_wealthy_starbolt.sql
nodes.summary: Adds liveness tracking columns (liveness_state, liveness_reason, continuation_attempt, last_useful_action_at, next_action) to the heartbeat_runs table and creates a supporting btree index for company-scoped liveness queries.

nodes.name: 0059_plugin_database_namespaces.sql
nodes.summary: Creates the plugin_database_namespaces and plugin_migrations tables, enabling plugins to manage their own isolated database namespaces and track migration history with status, checksums, and versioning.

nodes.name: plugin_database_namespaces
nodes.summary: Stores plugin-owned database namespace registrations, tracking namespace name, mode, and lifecycle status per plugin.

nodes.name: plugin_migrations
nodes.summary: Tracks migration files applied to plugin database namespaces, including checksum, plugin version, status, and error messages for safe idempotent migration management.

nodes.name: 0060_orange_annihilus.sql
nodes.summary: Creates the issue_reference_mentions table, which records cross-issue reference detection (source/target issue pairs, mention context, and document key), with unique constraints to prevent duplicate mention records.

nodes.name: issue_reference_mentions
nodes.summary: Stores detected cross-issue reference mentions, linking source and target issues with context about where and how the reference appeared (document key, matched text, source record).

nodes.name: 0061_lively_thor_girl.sql
nodes.summary: Adds scheduled retry tracking columns (scheduled_retry_at, scheduled_retry_attempt, scheduled_retry_reason) to the heartbeat_runs table to support deferred retry logic.

nodes.name: 0062_routine_run_dispatch_fingerprint.sql
nodes.summary: Adds dispatch_fingerprint to routine_runs and origin_fingerprint to issues, then rebuilds the routine execution uniqueness index to incorporate fingerprints for more granular deduplication of dispatched routine runs.

nodes.name: 0063_issue_thread_interactions.sql
nodes.summary: Creates the issue_thread_interactions table, capturing structured human-agent interactions within issue threads (approvals, questions, continuation policies) with full actor attribution and resolution tracking.

nodes.name: issue_thread_interactions
nodes.summary: Records structured interactions on issue threads (e.g., agent approval requests, user responses), tracking kind, status, continuation policy, associated run, actor attribution for both creation and resolution, and full payload/result.

nodes.name: 0064_issue_thread_interaction_idempotency.sql
nodes.summary: Adds a unique index on issue_thread_interactions to enforce idempotency by preventing duplicate interactions for the same company, issue, and idempotency key combination.

nodes.name: 0065_environments.sql
nodes.summary: Creates the environments and environment_leases tables, establishing the execution environment management subsystem with support for pluggable drivers, lease lifecycle tracking, and linkage to issues and heartbeat runs.

nodes.name: environments
nodes.summary: Defines execution environments with configurable drivers, storing name, description, driver type, status, config, and metadata per company.

nodes.name: environment_leases
nodes.summary: Tracks active and historical leases on execution environments, linking each lease to an environment, execution workspace, issue, and heartbeat run with full lifecycle timestamps and cleanup status.

nodes.name: 0066_issue_tree_holds.sql
nodes.summary: Creates issue_tree_holds and issue_tree_hold_members tables, implementing a mechanism to pause entire issue subtrees with actor attribution, release policy, and per-member status snapshots for coordinated hold/release operations.

nodes.name: issue_tree_holds
nodes.summary: Represents a hold placed on an issue tree rooted at root_issue_id, tracking mode, status, reason, release policy, and full actor attribution for both creation and release events.

nodes.name: issue_tree_hold_members
nodes.summary: Snapshots individual issues within an issue tree hold, capturing depth, parent linkage, assignment, active run, and skip status at the time the hold was created.

nodes.name: 0067_agent_default_environment.sql
nodes.summary: Adds a default_environment_id foreign key to the agents table, allowing each agent to be assigned a preferred execution environment by default.

nodes.name: 0068_environment_local_driver_unique.sql
nodes.summary: Replaces the environments company/driver index with a partial unique index ensuring only one local-driver environment can exist per company.

nodes.name: 0069_liveness_recovery_dedupe.sql
nodes.summary: Adds two partial unique indexes on the issues table to deduplicate liveness recovery incident and leaf issues by origin kind, id, and fingerprint, preventing concurrent duplicate recovery runs.

nodes.name: 0070_active_run_output_watchdog.sql
nodes.summary: Creates the heartbeat_run_watchdog_decisions table and adds supporting indexes to heartbeat_runs, implementing a watchdog subsystem that records snooze/action decisions for stale agent runs.

nodes.name: heartbeat_run_watchdog_decisions
nodes.summary: Records watchdog evaluation decisions for individual heartbeat runs, capturing the decision type, snooze deadline, reason, and full actor attribution (agent, user, or run).

nodes.name: 0071_default_hire_approval_off.sql
nodes.summary: Changes the default value of the require_board_approval_for_new_agents column on companies to false, disabling mandatory hire approval by default.

nodes.name: 0072_large_sandman.sql
nodes.summary: Adds a partial unique index on issues for stranded issue recovery deduplication, ensuring only one active recovery issue exists per origin kind/id combination.

nodes.name: 0073_shiny_salo.sql
nodes.summary: Adds the attachment_max_bytes column to the companies table with a default of 10 MB (10485760 bytes), enabling per-company attachment size limits.

nodes.name: 0074_striped_genesis.sql
nodes.summary: Adds a partial unique index on issues to deduplicate productivity review issues, ensuring only one active productivity review issue exists per origin kind/id combination.

nodes.name: 0075_cultured_sebastian_shaw.sql
nodes.summary: Adds monitor scheduling columns (monitor_next_check_at, monitor_wake_requested_at, monitor_last_triggered_at, monitor_attempt_count, monitor_notes, monitor_scheduled_by) to the issues table and creates a covering index for efficient monitor due-date queries.

nodes.name: 0076_useful_elektra.sql
nodes.summary: Creates the plugin_managed_resources table, allowing plugins to register and manage external resources (by kind and key) with default config, with a unique constraint per company/plugin/resource combination.

nodes.name: plugin_managed_resources
nodes.summary: Tracks external resources managed by plugins, identified by plugin key, resource kind, and resource key, with optional defaults JSON for provisioning configuration.

nodes.name: 0077_unusual_karnak.sql
nodes.summary: Creates the routine_revisions table and backfills initial revisions from existing routines, establishing a versioned snapshot history for routines with full actor attribution and self-referential restore tracking.

nodes.name: routine_revisions
nodes.summary: Stores versioned snapshots of routines as immutable revisions, supporting change summaries, restore-from-revision linkage, and full actor attribution (agent, user, or run) for audit trail and rollback.

nodes.name: 0078_white_darwin.sql
nodes.summary: Adds author_type, presentation, and metadata columns to issue_comments, enabling typed comment authorship and rich presentation metadata for agent-authored comments.

nodes.name: 0079_company_search_document_indexes.sql
nodes.summary: Creates GIN trigram indexes on documents.title and documents.latest_body to enable fast fuzzy full-text search across document content.

nodes.name: 0080_company_search_fuzzystrmatch.sql
nodes.summary: Installs the PostgreSQL fuzzystrmatch extension to enable phonetic and fuzzy string matching algorithms (Soundex, Levenshtein, Metaphone) for company search features.

nodes.name: 0081_optimal_dormammu.sql
nodes.summary: Adds the work_mode column to the issues table with a default value of 'standard', enabling per-issue work mode configuration for future execution mode differentiation.

nodes.name: 0082_dry_vision.sql
nodes.summary: Creates company_secret_bindings and secret_access_events tables, implementing the secrets management subsystem with binding of secrets to agents/plugins, version selectors, required flags, and a complete audit log of secret access events.

nodes.name: company_secret_bindings
nodes.summary: Binds company secrets to specific targets (agents, plugins, etc.) with config path mapping, version selector, required flag, and human-readable label for secret injection management.

nodes.name: secret_access_events
nodes.summary: Immutable audit log of every secret access event, capturing secret id, version, provider, actor (type and id), consumer (type and id), associated issue/run/plugin, and outcome for compliance and debugging.

nodes.name: company_secret_provider_configs
nodes.summary: Defines the company_secret_provider_configs table storing per-company secret provider configurations (e.g., vault, cloud KMS) with health-check tracking, default flag, and audit timestamps; also migrates company_secrets.provider_config_id to a proper UUID FK.

nodes.name: 0083_company_secret_provider_configs.sql
nodes.summary: Migration 0083: creates the company_secret_provider_configs table with health-check columns and FK constraints to companies and agents; migrates the provider_config_id column in company_secrets to a typed UUID with a FK.

nodes.name: issue_recovery_actions
nodes.summary: Defines the issue_recovery_actions table that tracks automated recovery attempts for issues, capturing owner (agent/user), cause, fingerprint, wake/monitor policies, attempt counters, timeouts, and resolution state.

nodes.name: 0084_issue_recovery_actions.sql
nodes.summary: Migration 0084: creates the issue_recovery_actions table with FK constraints to companies, issues, and agents (owner, previous_owner, return_owner), and composite unique indexes that enforce one active recovery per source issue and fingerprint.
